title: Planning, Dependencies, and Adaptive Decomposition · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: Plan a migration whose dependencies and risks emerge during work. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a dependency-aware adaptive execution plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Order outcomes by prerequisite evidence rather than arbitrary file sequence.
Choose milestones that leave a verified, restartable artifact.
Update the plan when evidence changes while preserving the goal and recorded decisions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Bounded agent-loop portfolio. No external effects occur in this browser lab."""

steps.title: Dependency Graph · changed case
steps.instruction:

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Plan User Choice applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Pause for user direction only when missing authority or a choice materially changes the outcome.
